Rule.name: Application Connections Health Check Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is used to test the application Connections

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Signature:
me sirf do inputs declare kiye hain: log aur context. XML
... ...
Agar aap is Rule ko direct kisi Task se chalayenge aur wahan se sendMail ya workGroup pass karenge, toh Beanshell unhe automatically config ke andar daal dega.Lekin standard practice yeh kehti hai ki jo bhi input aap config se nikal rahe hain, unhe upar ke andar bhi as an declare kar dena chahiye taaki dekhne wale ko pata rahe ki ish Rule ko kya-kya inputs chahiye.
()case1:-matlab koi bhi xml1st me aur xml2nd me dennge toh sailpoint isshe config me dal dega aur xml2nd se xm1st ko call kare ya xml1st se xm2nd ko call kare config se hum dono me aek dusre ka pass kar sakte hai.
()Case 2: Agar aap ek Rule (XML 1) se doosre Rule (XML 2) ko call kar rahe hain Yahan par aakar twist badal jata hai. Agar aap ek Rule ke andar baith kar context.runRule() ya context.runExtensionRule() use karke doosre Rule ka naam call kar rahe hain, toh SailPoint automatically config ko pass nahi karta. Wahan aapko khud hath se (explicitly) data pass karna padta hai. ex:- Jab aap ek Rule se doosre Rule ko call karte hain, toh aap ek HashMap banate hain aur usme saare arguments daal kar bhejte hain:- 🔴 Rule 1 (XML 1) ke andar ka code: Java // Ek naya jola (Map) banaya data bhejne ke liye Map ruleArgs = new HashMap(); ruleArgs.put("sendMail", "false"); ruleArgs.put("workGroup", "MyAwesomeWorkgroup"); // Doosre rule (XML 2) ko call kiya aur ruleArgs pass kar diya Rule targetRule = context.getObjectByName(Rule.class, "MySecondRuleName"); Object result = context.runRule(targetRule, ruleArgs);
🟢 Rule 2 (XML 2) ke andar aap use kaise nikalenge:- Jab Rule 1 ne ruleArgs bheja, toh Rule 2 ke paas wo saare variables direct variable name se ya fir config se mil jayenge:- Java // Rule 2 me aap isse direct nikal sakte hain:- String mailSetting = (String) config.get("sendMail"); // ya fir Beanshell me direct variable ki tarah: // String mailSetting = sendMail; *//////// ////////////* ()Simple Copy (Shallow Copy): Agar aap bina deepCopy kiye direct template me koi badlav (changes) karte hain, toh wo badlav SailPoint ke database (cache) me jo asli template hai, usme permanent change ho jayega. Iska matlab, agar is Rule ne template badla, toh poore SailPoint me sabhi ke liye wo template badal jayega, jo ki ek bohot bada disaster (gadbad) ho sakta hai.
()Deep Copy: deepCopy(context) kya karta hai? Ye database se aaye asli EmailTemplate ka ek ekdum naya, bilkul alag aur duplicate copy memory (RAM) me bana deta hai. */////////////
////////////* ()Kya ise ek line me likh sakte hain? Haan, bilkul likh sakte hain! Java ko isse koi farq nahi padta. Agar aap ise bina tode ek single line me likhenge, toh ye bilkul sahi chalega:- Java String APPLICATION_NAMES = "select name from identityiq.spt_application where name not like 'Test_%' and name not like '%Demo%' and type not in ('DelimitedFile','IdentityIQ Workgroups')"; ⚠️ Ek Badi Galti (Space Trap): Aapke code me pehli line %'_ par khatam ho rahi hai aur doosri line "and se shuru ho rahi hai. Jab Java in dono ko jodega, toh wo ban jayega '%Demo%'and type. Dekhiye '%Demo%' aur and ke beech ka space gayab ho gaya! Is wajah se SQL query database me fail ho sakti hai. Hamesha doosri line ke shuru me ek space dena chahiye, jaise: " and type...".
2. Kya SQL likhne ka koi fixed sequence (tarteeb) hota hai? Haan, SQL ka ek ekdum fixed sequence hota hai. Database aapke "words" ko tabhi samajhta hai jab wo ek proper standard sequence me likhe hon. Aap is sequence ko aage-piche nahi kar sakte.Standard SQL Query ka sequence yeh hota hai:-
Sequence Order Keyword Aapke Code Me Kya Likha Hai Kaam (Purpose) 1 (Sabse Pehle)----------SELECT----------------select name-------------------Kaun sa column chahiye? (Hume sirf 'name' chahiye). 2-------------------------FROM------------from identityiq.spt_application----------Kaun se table se data nikalna hai? 3-------------------------WHERE-------------where name not like...-----------------Data filter karne ki pehli condition. 4-------------------------AND / OR----------and type not in...------------Pehli condition ke saath doosri extra condition jodna.
*////////////
